intro: Icacorea pluriflora, also known as the Icacurana or Icaco tree, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique, showy flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Icacorea pluriflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and infusions-based extracts, as well as being used in folk medicine for its medicinal and ritualistic purposes.
